ddls:
CREATE TABLE "probability_distributions" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each probability distribution */,
  "range" TEXT /* Range of the probability distribution (e.g., Less than -61.123%) */,
  "frequency" INTEGER /* Frequency of the probability distribution */,
  "probability" REAL /* Probability of the probability distribution */,
  "cumulative_percentage" REAL /* Cumulative percentage of the probability distribution */,
  "distribution_type" TEXT /* Type of the probability distribution (e.g., normal, uniform, binomial) */,
  "distribution_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key linking to the distribution parameters */,
  "portfolio_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key linking to the investment portfolio */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "investment_portfolios" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each investment portfolio */,
  "portfolio_name" TEXT /* Name of the investment portfolio */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the investment portfolio */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the portfolio was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "distribution_parameters" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each set of distribution parameters */,
  "distribution_type" TEXT /* Type of the probability distribution (e.g., normal, uniform, binomial) */,
  "mean" REAL /* Mean of the distribution */,
  "standard_deviation" REAL /* Standard deviation of the distribution */,
  "sample_size" INTEGER /* Sample size of the distribution */,
  "min_value" REAL /* Minimum value for uniform distribution */,
  "max_value" REAL /* Maximum value for uniform distribution */,
  "probability_of_success" REAL /* Probability of success for binomial distribution */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "calculations" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each calculation */,
  "distribution_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key linking to the probability distribution */,
  "calculation_type" TEXT /* Type of the calculation (e.g., cumulative_percentage, potential_loss) */,
  "result" REAL /* Result of the calculation */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the calculation was performed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "events" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each extreme event */,
  "event_name" TEXT /* Name of the extreme event */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the extreme event */,
  "probability" REAL /* Probability of the extreme event occurring */,
  "impact" REAL /* Impact of the extreme event (e.g., financial loss) */,
  "distribution_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key linking to the probability distribution */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the event was recorded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)
